description: Write protection rules defining operations that are always denied, require human approval, or are managed files with strict modification constraints.

always_deny.description: These operations are always denied for AI agents — no exceptions.

rules.reason: Destructive deletion
Force push can lose history
Destructive database operation
Bulk data deletion
Disk formatting
Force remove running containers
Insecure permissions
Environment files may contain secrets — never written or modified by agents
Files matching 'secret' pattern are never read or written by agents
Credential files are never read or written by agents
Token files are never read or written by agents

human_gate.description: These operations always require explicit human approval before execution. Each gate is action-specific, scope-specific, and non-transferable.

gates.description: Before any file changes in the target project
Before changes are pushed to remote
Before a pull request is created
gates.reason: Prevent AI from creating PRs autonomously
Prevent AI from merging PRs autonomously

gates.description: Before remote CI is enabled or executed
gates.reason: Remote CI may consume resources or expose secrets

gates.description: Before a Tier 2 MCP server is activated or used
gates.reason: Tier 2 MCPs have full access and require human oversight

gates.description: Before agents create or modify skills
gates.reason: Skill changes can affect agent behavior across sessions

gates.description: Before agents write to memory files
gates.reason: Memory writes persist across sessions and may propagate incorrect context

existing_rules.description: Legacy human_gate_required patterns preserved for backward compatibility

rules.reason: Prevent accidental remote pushes
Prevent AI from closing issues autonomously
Prevent AI from merging PRs autonomously
Prevent production deployment
Prevent package publishing
Prevent release tagging

managed_files.description: Configuration, agent, policy, and documentation files that require strict modification constraints.

rules.reason: Configuration files affect all agent behavior and must be changed with care
Agent definition files control delegation and capability boundaries
Policy files define security, compliance, and evidence boundaries
Documentation files define project rules and must be kept accurate
CI workflows may execute remote code, consume resources, and expose secrets

production_data.description: Production database operations always require human gate

file_protection.description: Legacy file protection rules — superseded by managed_files section but kept for tool-level enforcement
